picks.name: Microsoft Corporation
picks.thesis: MSFT is down 34% from its 52-week high while earnings grew 12%/yr — a sentiment-driven pullback, not fundamental deterioration. Quality looks intact (healthy margins, durable revenue, long-term uptrend), and it's now at or below fair value ($368.58 vs $446.05, low confidence). Valued with DCF and Comps.

methods.name: DDM — dividend discount
methods.reason: Not meaningful: this is a growth-oriented company that reinvests and returns cash mainly via buybacks rather than dividends (yield 0.9%, payout 25%), so a dividend-only model understates it — DDM excluded

reconciliation.sentence: The methods land between $129.58 and $762.52 (a 488% spread). They disagree sharply, so confidence is low — treat the value as a wide range.

why: low confidence across 2 models — DCF $129.58, Comps $762.52
profile: ✓ → multiple compression: price down 34% while earnings grew +12%/yr (sentiment, not deterioration)
